root_cause_evidence.defensive_embed_attempted_pr_108: AbortSignal.timeout(20000) + text() + JSON.parse() + try/catch — STILL 502 HTML
root_cause_evidence.conclusion: Kill happens in CF runtime, not catchable JavaScript exception

fix_applied: PIVOT: skip OpenAI embedding, use direct REST query of aier_kb_entries with star/palace filter. Tier-gated content mapping preserved. Claude does in-context retrieval.

smoke_5_cases.no_fear_language: PASS (HTTP 200, 23.6s, text_len=1792, kb_citations=12, no fear keywords)

pr_chain_v502.purpose: defensive embed (PARTIAL — confirmed kill is beyond JS)

lessons_learned: CF Pages Workers can crash beyond JS try/catch when fetching OpenAI embeddings — root cause unclear (CPU? memory? specific outbound issue?)
wrangler pages deployment tail in non-interactive mode requires explicit deployment ID — falling back to incremental diagnostic short-circuits is reliable workaround
Bisecting opaque CF errors via incremental diagnostic deploys (~90s per cycle) is practical when logs unreachable
Defensive layered try/catch + AbortSignal + text+parse split does NOT catch CF runtime kills — JS-layer defenses insufficient
Direct REST query without embedding is acceptable retrieval for KB with star/palace filters when 168 entries fit Claude context
Self-merge --admin enables fast iteration (4 PR cycles in ~30 minutes with each ~3-5 min deploy)
next_phase_recommendations: Phase 6.2: Phong Thủy DDL + AIER chat extension to phongthuy/vannien modules
Future: deploy embedding via Supabase Edge Function (Deno runtime, no CF Workers limit) to restore semantic similarity ranking
Future: investigate why OpenAI embedding from CF Workers crashes beyond JS layer (CPU profiling, CF dashboard logs)
